root_text.an10.36:1.1: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, dhammehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo”ti?
root_text.an10.36:1.2: “Dasahi kho, upāli, dhammehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo.
root_text.an10.36:1.4: Idhupāli, bhikkhu sīlavā hoti …pe… samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu;
root_text.an10.36:1.6: pātimokkhaṁ kho panassa vitthārena svāgataṁ hoti suvibhattaṁ suppavattaṁ suvinicchitaṁ suttaso anubyañjanaso;
root_text.an10.36:1.7: paṭibalo hoti gilānaṁ upaṭṭhātuṁ vā upaṭṭhāpetuṁ vā;
root_text.an10.36:1.8: paṭibalo hoti anabhiratiṁ vūpakāsetuṁ vā vūpakāsāpetuṁ vā;
root_text.an10.36:1.9: paṭibalo hoti uppannaṁ kukkuccaṁ dhammato vinodetuṁ;
root_text.an10.36:1.10: paṭibalo hoti uppannaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ dhammato vivecetuṁ;
root_text.an10.36:1.14: Imehi kho, upāli, dasahi dhammehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo”ti.

translation_text.an10.36:1.1: “Sir, how many qualities should a mendicant have to be attended on by a novice?”
translation_text.an10.36:1.2: “Upāli, a mendicant should have ten qualities to be attended on by a novice.
translation_text.an10.36:1.4: It’s when a mendicant is ethical …
translation_text.an10.36:1.6: Both monastic codes have been passed down to them in detail, well analyzed, well mastered, well evaluated in both the rules and accompanying material.
translation_text.an10.36:1.7: They’re able to care for the sick or get someone else to do so.
translation_text.an10.36:1.8: They’re able to settle dissatisfaction or get someone else to do so.
translation_text.an10.36:1.9: They’re able to dispel remorse when it has come up.
translation_text.an10.36:1.10: They’re able to rationally dissuade someone from misconceptions that come up.
translation_text.an10.36:1.11: They’re able to encourage someone in the higher ethics,
translation_text.an10.36:1.14: A mendicant should possess these ten qualities to be attended on by a novice.”
